items.briefing:
The Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) has announced it will retire the Essential Eight cybersecurity framework within the next two years and is currently consulting on a replacement. If your organisation follows the Essential Eight for compliance or security guidance, now is a good time to engage with ASD's consultation process and stay informed about what the new framework will require.

In international news, members of the Scattered Spider hacking group pleaded guilty on the first day of their US trial. This group was responsible for major corporate breaches and used social engineering tactics that could target any organisation. **Practical tip:** Remind staff to verify unexpected IT requests by phone before acting on them, no matter how convincing they seem.

featured.title: ASD to retire Essential Eight within two years, consults on replacement
featured.quote: Australia's foundational cybersecurity framework is being replaced after two decades of guidance.
